lastElection.notes: United Russia won 324/450 seats.

nextElection.notes: Scheduled September 2026.

lastElection.notes: Putin re-elected with reported 87% of vote.
BJP-led NDA won majority; Modi began third term as PM.
Droupadi Murmu re-confirmed via parliamentary vote.
PTI-backed independents won most seats; PML-N formed coalition government.
Asif Ali Zardari elected president by parliament.

dataAvailability.executive: Xi Jinping holds supreme authority as General Secretary of the CCP and CMC Chairman. Li Qiang is Premier (head of government).

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: One-party state. The Chinese Communist Party holds a monopoly on political power. National People's Congress 'elections' are uncontested single-party votes.

lastElection.notes: NPC delegates selected through party-controlled process. Not a competitive election.
Xi Jinping confirmed for third term by NPC; no opposition candidates permitted.

metadata.officialName: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

lastElection.notes: Labour won a landslide majority (412/650 seats). Keir Starmer became PM.

nextElection.notes: Due by January 2029 under the Dissolution and Calling of Parliament Act 2022.

lastElection.notes: PM is appointed after winning a parliamentary majority.

dataAvailability.executive: CDU/CSU won the February 2025 snap election; Merz became Chancellor in April 2025 after coalition negotiations.

lastElection.notes: CDU/CSU won 28.5%; SPD 16.4%. AfD came second with 20.8%. Merz formed CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition.
Chancellor is elected by the Bundestag following federal elections.

headOfState.name: Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan

headOfGovernment.name: Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum

executiveInPower.leader: Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum

bodies.name: Federal National Council
bodies.inControl: Non-partisan (advisory body; half appointed, half indirectly elected)

dataAvailability.executive: UAE is a federal monarchy; executive positions are hereditary. No public elections for president or PM.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Federal constitutional monarchy. Executive positions are hereditary. The Federal National Council is half-appointed, half indirectly elected via a limited electorate.

lastElection.notes: Only half of the 40-seat FNC is indirectly elected by an appointed electorate of ~300,000. The body is advisory only.

dataAvailability.executive: King Salman is head of state; Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (MBS) serves as Prime Minister and is de facto ruler.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Absolute monarchy with no national elections for executive or legislative positions. The Consultative Assembly (Majlis al-Shura) is fully appointed by royal decree.

lastElection.notes: Netanyahu's Likud-led bloc won 64/120 seats.

nextElection.notes: Legal deadline. Netanyahu may call snap elections earlier (as early as mid-2026).

lastElection.notes: PM emerges from parliamentary coalition formation after elections.

dataAvailability.executive: Mahmoud Abbas rules by decree since elections were last held in 2006 (legislative) and 2005 (presidential). PM Mohammad Mustafa was appointed March 2024.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: World Bank data for Palestine is limited due to its political status.
dataAvailability.elections: No elections since 2006 (legislative) and 2005 (presidential). Mahmoud Abbas rules by decree.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: No elections held since 2006 (legislative) and 2005 (presidential). Mahmoud Abbas rules by decree; Hamas controls Gaza. Elections indefinitely postponed.

lastElection.notes: Hamas won 74/132 seats. No elections held since; council suspended.
Mahmoud Abbas elected. No subsequent presidential election held.

dataAvailability.executive: Mexico has a presidential system. Sheinbaum became Mexico's first elected female president in October 2024.

lastElection.notes: Morena coalition won supermajority (364/500 seats in Chamber).
Claudia Sheinbaum won with ~59% of vote.

nextElection.notes: Mexico has a single 6-year term (sexenio) with no re-election.

lastElection.notes: PL (Bolsonaro's party) won most Chamber seats despite Lula winning presidency.

nextElection.notes: Both congressional and presidential elections held simultaneously in October 2026.

lastElection.notes: Lula defeated Bolsonaro 50.9% to 49.1% in a runoff.

nextElection.notes: Lula eligible for re-election. October 2026.

dataAvailability.executive: Mark Carney became PM on 14 March 2025 after Trudeau's resignation. Liberals won the 28 April 2025 election with a minority (169 seats).

lastElection.notes: Liberals won 169/343 seats (minority). Carney's Liberals won amid Trump tariff tensions.

nextElection.notes: Due by October 2029 at latest; minority government makes earlier election possible.

lastElection.type: Federal election (PM selected by House majority)
lastElection.notes: PM is appointed following federal election result.
APC retained majorities in both chambers.
Tinubu won with 36.6% in a fractured three-way race.

dataAvailability.executive: Sanae Takaichi became Japan's first female PM in October 2025 after winning the LDP leadership race. She called a snap election on 8 February 2026, winning a historic LDP supermajority.

lastElection.notes: LDP won historic supermajority of 316/465 seats. First snap election called by Takaichi as PM.
PM emerges from the parliamentary majority. Takaichi confirmed.

dataAvailability.executive: Ali Khamenei was killed on 28 February 2026 in US/Israeli strikes. A three-member interim council (President, Chief Justice, Guardian Council rep) is managing the transition. A new Supreme Leader election by the Assembly of Experts is expected within 90 days.

lastElection.notes: Conservatives/Principlists won majority in low-turnout election (~41%). Second round April 2024.
Masoud Pezeshkian (reformist) won snap election after Raisi's death in helicopter crash.

nextElection.notes: Assembly of Experts will also elect a new Supreme Leader — timeline uncertain.

dataAvailability.executive: Assad fled in December 2024. Ahmad al-Sharaa (formerly Abu Mohammad al-Jolani) leads the transitional government. No functioning legislature.
dataAvailability.elections: No elections scheduled. Transitional authority governing.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Transitional authority following Assad's fall (December 2024). No elections scheduled; country is governed by interim administration (Hayat Tahrir al-Sham).

lastElection.notes: Final Assad-era election, held during civil war. Not recognised as legitimate.

bodies.name: National Assembly
bodies.inControl: No single majority (hung parliament; left-wing NFP largest bloc)

dataAvailability.executive: François Bayrou became PM in January 2025 after Michel Barnier's government fell on a no-confidence vote in December 2024.

lastElection.notes: Left-wing NFP won most seats but no majority. Macron's Ensemble third. Hung parliament.

nextElection.notes: Next statutory election due 2029. Snap election possible if government falls again.

lastElection.notes: Macron defeated Le Pen 58.5% to 41.5%.

nextElection.notes: Macron is term-limited; cannot run again.

dataAvailability.executive: Turkey has a presidential system since 2018; Erdoğan is both head of state and government.

lastElection.notes: AKP and allies retained parliamentary majority.
Erdoğan defeated Kılıçdaroğlu 52% to 48% in runoff.

nextElection.notes: Constitutional two-term limit — Erdoğan may seek a third term via snap election reset.

dataAvailability.executive: Nicolás Maduro fled Venezuela in late 2025; VP Delcy Rodríguez became acting president on 3 January 2026. Edmundo González is internationally recognised by some states as legitimate president-elect (July 2024 election disputed).

lastElection.notes: Opposition boycotted. PSUV won 253/277 seats in discredited election.
Maduro declared winner; results internationally disputed. Opposition's González widely recognised as actual winner.

nextElection.notes: If current government holds power; legitimacy deeply contested.

dataAvailability.executive: Lương Cường became President in October 2024. General Secretary Tô Lâm holds supreme authority as party leader.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: One-party socialist republic. The Communist Party of Vietnam controls all state institutions. National Assembly candidates are vetted by the party.

lastElection.notes: All candidates vetted by Vietnam Fatherland Front (CPV body). 99.6% voter turnout reported.

nextElection.notes: Scheduled May 2026.

lastElection.notes: President elected by National Assembly on CPV nomination.

bodies.name: Legislative Yuan
bodies.inControl: KMT and Taiwan People's Party (TPP) hold combined majority

dataAvailability.executive: Taiwan is not a UN member state; sovereignty disputed by China. Lai Ching-te (William Lai) became president in May 2024.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Taiwan is not a UN member and is not recognised by the World Bank. WGI data unavailable.
dataAvailability.elections: Taiwan is not an IPU member (non-UN member state). Election data from static records.

lastElection.notes: DPP lost its majority. KMT won most seats; TPP holds balance of power.
Lai Ching-te won with 40.1% in three-way race.

dataAvailability.executive: Lee Jae-myung won the June 2025 snap presidential election after Yoon Suk-yeol's impeachment and removal for his December 2024 martial law attempt.

lastElection.notes: Democratic Party won 175/300 seats (majority). Strong rebuke of Yoon government.
Lee Jae-myung won after Yoon Suk-yeol's impeachment for declaring martial law in December 2024.

nextElection.notes: South Korea has a single 5-year presidential term with no re-election.

dataAvailability.executive: Kim Jong-un holds supreme authority as General Secretary of the KWP, President of State Affairs, and Supreme Commander of the armed forces.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: North Korea governance data is based on limited external assessments.
dataAvailability.elections: North Korea holds nominal single-party elections not tracked by IPU as competitive.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Totalitarian single-party state. Supreme People's Assembly elections feature a single Korean Workers' Party-approved candidate per seat with near-100% reported turnout.

lastElection.notes: Single-party slate with a single candidate per constituency. 99.9% turnout reported. Not a competitive election.

dataAvailability.executive: Prabowo Subianto became president in October 2024, succeeding Joko Widodo (Jokowi).

lastElection.notes: Golkar, Gerindra and PDI-P led in DPR seats. Simultaneous with presidential vote.
Prabowo won 58.6% in a three-candidate race.

nextElection.notes: Indonesia has a two-term limit; Prabowo eligible for one more term.

metadata.officialName: Republic of the Union of Myanmar

dataAvailability.executive: Myanmar has been under military junta (State Administration Council) rule since the February 2021 coup. The elected NLD government operates in exile as the National Unity Government.
dataAvailability.elections: No elections scheduled. Military junta (SAC) suspended parliament after February 2021 coup.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Military junta (State Administration Council) since the February 2021 coup. The elected NLD government operates in exile as the National Unity Government.

lastElection.notes: NLD won landslide (396/476 seats). Military nullified results and staged coup on 1 Feb 2021.
Last legitimate election before coup.
Civil Contract won 54% and majority. Called after post-war protests.

nextElection.notes: Due by 2026. Pashinyan may call snap election following Armenia-Azerbaijan peace deal controversy.

lastElection.notes: Vahagn Khachaturyan elected by National Assembly. Ceremonial role.
New Azerbaijan Party won 68/100 seats. OSCE reported significant irregularities.
Aliyev won 92.1%. No meaningful opposition permitted.
RNI won 102/395 seats and formed a coalition government.

nextElection.notes: Due September 2026.

dataAvailability.executive: Somalia has a fragile federal government with indirect elections via clan delegates. Hassan Sheikh Mohamud was re-elected by parliament in May 2022.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Somalia governance data based on limited external assessments due to conflict.

lastElection.notes: Indirect clan-delegate system. No universal suffrage. Process completed late 2021 / early 2022.

nextElection.notes: Expected 2025–2026. A move towards direct universal suffrage elections has been discussed but not implemented.

lastElection.notes: Hassan Sheikh Mohamud elected by parliament, 214/328 votes.

dataAvailability.executive: Yemen is split between Houthi-controlled north and the internationally recognised government. The Presidential Leadership Council (est. 2022) chairs the recognised government.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Yemen governance data from internationally recognised government baseline; actual governance severely disrupted by civil war.
dataAvailability.elections: No elections possible. Country divided between Houthi and internationally recognised government.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Civil war. Two parallel governments (Houthi/Ansar Allah in the north; Presidential Leadership Council in the south). No elections possible.

lastElection.notes: Last parliamentary election before civil war. Parliament technically still in session but non-functional.
Abd Rabbuh Mansur Hadi won uncontested election during Arab Spring transition. Replaced by Presidential Leadership Council in 2022.

dataAvailability.executive: Libya has two rival governments: the UN-recognised Government of National Unity (GNU) in Tripoli, and the rival government backed by the House of Representatives in Benghazi/Tobruk.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Libya has parallel governing authorities; data reflects the internationally recognised GNU.
dataAvailability.elections: No elections held since 2014. Planned 2021 elections cancelled.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Divided state with two rival governments. Elections scheduled for 2021 were never held. No current electoral process.

lastElection.notes: Low turnout (18%). Led to political split and parallel governments.

bodies.name: House of Representatives
bodies.inControl: Pro-Sisi independents and Nation's Future Party

lastElection.notes: Nation's Future Party and pro-Sisi independents won overwhelmingly. Opposition largely excluded.

nextElection.notes: Due 2025. Exact date not yet confirmed.

lastElection.notes: Sisi won 89.6% in a tightly controlled election. No meaningful opposition.
FLN and allies won majority in low-turnout election (~23%).
Tebboune re-elected with 94.7% in a low-turnout election.

bodies.name: Chamber of Deputies
bodies.inControl: No single majority (Peronist blocs largest; Milei minority)

lastElection.notes: Half of Chamber renewed. Milei's LLA made strong gains; Peronists retained largest bloc.

nextElection.notes: Half of Chamber of Deputies renewed in October 2025.

lastElection.notes: Milei defeated Massa (Peronist) 55.7% to 44.3%.

nextElection.notes: Argentina has a 4-year term with one re-election permitted.

lastElection.notes: Right-wing Chile Vamos won most seats; Boric's coalition controls government despite congress opposition.

nextElection.notes: Boric is term-limited; cannot stand for re-election.

lastElection.notes: Gabriel Boric defeated José Antonio Kast 55.9% to 44.1%.

nextElection.notes: Boric cannot run again (single 4-year term). Election November 2025.

bodies.name: Congress
bodies.inControl: Right-wing and centre-right coalition (Alliance for Progress largest bloc)

dataAvailability.executive: Dina Boluarte became president in December 2022 after Pedro Castillo's failed self-coup and impeachment. She was previously VP.

lastElection.notes: Highly fragmented result. No party won more than 15% of seats.

nextElection.notes: April 2026. Boluarte is serving out Castillo's term.

lastElection.notes: Castillo defeated Fujimori 50.1% to 49.9%. Castillo impeached Dec 2022; Boluarte assumed presidency.

nextElection.notes: April 2026. Boluarte cannot run (completing prior term, not her own).

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: One-party socialist republic. The Communist Party of Cuba is the only legal party. National Assembly candidates are pre-approved.

lastElection.notes: Single-slate election. All 470 candidates pre-approved by Communist Party.
Díaz-Canel re-confirmed by National Assembly.

bodies.name: House of Representatives
bodies.inControl: Fragmented (Pacto Histórico minority; no clear majority)

lastElection.notes: Pacto Histórico won most seats but not a majority.

nextElection.notes: March 2026 — congressional election precedes presidential.

lastElection.notes: Petro defeated Hernández 50.4% to 47.3%. First left-wing president in Colombia's history.

nextElection.notes: Petro is term-limited (single 4-year term). May 2026.

bodies.name: National Assembly
bodies.inControl: Fragmented (no single majority; Realizing Goals is largest party)

lastElection.notes: Fragmented result. Mulino's party won plurality in Assembly.
Mulino won with 34.3% in a six-candidate race. Contested but upheld.

nextElection.notes: Panama has a single 5-year term with no re-election.

dataAvailability.executive: Nayib Bukele was re-elected in February 2024 despite constitutional single-term limits; the Supreme Court approved his candidacy in a controversial ruling.

lastElection.notes: New Ideas won 54/60 seats (supermajority). Bukele re-elected with ~85%.
Bukele won ~85% in a non-competitive race after sidelining opposition.

nextElection.notes: Constitutional status of future terms uncertain given court rulings on term limits.

dataAvailability.executive: King Frederik X succeeded Queen Margrethe II on 14 January 2024. PM Frederiksen called a snap election for 24 March 2026, capitalising on popularity from her stance against Trump's Greenland threats.

lastElection.notes: Left-wing bloc won narrow majority. Frederiksen formed minority government.

nextElection.notes: Called by Frederiksen 4 weeks early amid strong polling on Greenland sovereignty issue.

lastElection.notes: PM emerges from parliamentary majority formation.

dataAvailability.executive: Sudan has been in civil war since April 2023 between the SAF (al-Burhan) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF/Hemeti). The civilian transitional framework has collapsed.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Sudan is in active civil war since April 2023; governance data from pre-war assessments.
dataAvailability.elections: No elections. Military junta controls territory contested with RSF.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Military junta. Sudan is under Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) control amid civil war with the RSF. No elections scheduled or possible under current conditions.

bodies.name: Verkhovna Rada
bodies.inControl: Servant of the People (majority; elections suspended)

dataAvailability.executive: Elections are suspended under martial law due to the ongoing Russian invasion. Zelensky's presidential term was extended per wartime provisions; his term legally expired May 2024.
dataAvailability.elections: Elections suspended under martial law (Russian invasion ongoing since February 2022).

lastElection.notes: Servant of the People won supermajority (254/450 seats). Elections suspended under martial law since 2022.
Zelensky defeated Poroshenko 73% to 25%. Elections suspended under martial law; term extended by parliament.

electionDataModel.description: Each country's elections block contains competitiveElections (bool), nonCompetitiveReason (str|null for one-party/non-democratic states), and legislative/executive sub-blocks each with lastElection and nextElection. Election objects contain: date (ISO string or year), type (string description), notes (context string).
